texto: Most of the words we use in history and everyday speech are like mental depth charges. As they descend [through our consciousness] and detonate, their resonant power is unleashed, showering our understanding with fragments of accumulated meaning and association.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a James Axtell: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Most of the words we use in history and everyday speech are like mental depth charges. As they de…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, epiphany, history pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e history.
